page: Microsoft Azure F1 is an entry-level virtual machine within the F-family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor clocked at 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is limited to a bundled 16 GB volume, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks place the F1 instance consistently in the bottom 10% of cloud servers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both scored 804.38, reflecting the limitations of a single-threaded allocation. Memory bandwidth is constrained, with read-only small block cached operations reaching 75.04 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidth drop to 6.86 GB/sec and 5.25 GB/sec respectively. Passmark CPU and memory scores are similarly low, at 1187.46 and 762.45.

Given the low resource density and poor benchmark results, the F1 instance is suited primarily for lightweight, non-critical workloads. These include basic static web serving, which benchmarked at 1.05 GB/sec, or low-demand Redis key-value operations, which achieved 1.22 million SET operations per second. The instance is a low-cost entry point for testing, basic scripting, or small-scale utility tasks where high computational throughput and memory capacity are not required.
description: Microsoft Azure F1 is an entry-level virtual machine in the F-family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with a single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 core running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of RAM and 16 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Across synthetic benchmarks, the F1 consistently ranks in the bottom 10% of tested instances, with a stress-ng CPU score of 804.38 and a Passmark CPU score of 1187.46. Memory performance is also modest, yielding a large-block read bandwidth of 6.86 GB/sec. This instance provides a low-cost, minimal-resource environment suitable for basic utility tasks, lightweight static web serving, or simple development and testing workloads that do not demand high memory or parallel processing capabilities.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F1 is a single-core virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 16 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F1 is a single-vCPU virtual machine with 2.0 GB of RAM and 16 GB of local storage, suitable for basic utility tasks.
tagline: A single-core, low-resource virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for basic utility and testing workloads.

2.0 GB of system memory and 16 GB of local storage
x86_64 architecture running on Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
